column19: Scheme Twists in the Villain Deck
column23: Comments
How I love when Cloak and Daggers rare sees play <3
Civil War isn't really one of my favorite sets overall, but the times it comes together it can be really fun to play.

Went with the Avengers, and didn't use Loki much. Was lucky with Cap's rare in the HQ right at the beginning - so I knew how to ramp up for the difficult Final Blow; my last turn produced 33/// too bad I had that early Escape. Still a pretty nice game. The second try went worse and I even lost that match.
Had an absolutely incredible final turn!! Ended up playing Daredevil's rare for infinite attack, as the right side had exactly 2 cards of different costs in their discard pile!!
iron man + hawk eye destroyed this game

Okay, so M + Sif's Helm is bonkers, she casually drew her whole deck every turn, resending all Brainstorms back out of time and generating 20+ attack (and i didnt even get her rares). Tech side didnt join the game at all, but oh well.

2nd game went MUCH better, as Weaponized Galactus didn’t sit in the city and devour guardians of Asgard. M and Rogue were a force together, with some Sif’s Winged helm working to fully power Penance form with a 2-card draw to boot. Other deck had Team “Hawk” with Darkhawk and Hawkeye with amazing hyperspeed cycling. Though we are worried about our rather low VP total, didn’t clear much of the city toward the end.
Two weeks in a row I got location heavy set ups, but both felt different and unique. Felt a lot better about this weeks. Though the initial three heroes were tough to wrap my mind around.

column2: The Supreme Intellegence's Hidden Heart of Darkness
column23: Shortest game ever. my right’s 3rd hand had hulk uncommon and hulk common and two troopers and that was enough to hit the mastermind on turn 5

Went for lots of recruit plus a 4+ theme. Made lots of use out of  Wound Play with Strong Guy, Human Torch, and Luke. Fun strategically using wounds with two hands both having copies of Unbreakable Cage.

Great Scheme. I’ll have to remember it for teaching new players the game because of how quick it can end! M was an amazing choice for her cloning abilities 😍 Thank you for holding this tourney Bagel 🙌🏾
Hulk side and X-Men/X-Force side. Went astonishingly well. Got Karma's rare early because of Bishop's KOs for recruit and got 9 attack from it every time. Very fun!
In an act of karmic retribution, if I had let the game go on one more turn, I would have lost.
This looks deceptively easy, until villain deck decides you shall not pass

I focused on Teleport/Covert/X-Men on one hand and then Hulk/X-Force on the other. Cable never showed up in the first game at all, but in the second I managed to get 2 copies of "Strike at the Heart of Evil" into the same hand by turn 3, but then Skaar's Rare clogged up the Prison ship. Both games I drew the 7th Twist to lose the game, while having more than enough attack to strike the final blow on that turn. I had an 9 in 11 chance of winning on my submitted results (first game). Teleporting a couple turns in a row and then hitting hard was an effective strategy.

column2: Charles Xavier's Danger Room Goes Berserk
column23: First off new respect for Lightshow keyword, was interesting as I was someone who despised Lightshow, it is a good keyword, was a fun setup.

Didn’t see a single rare until the last couple turns of my second game! But the Thors kept the neurotoxins at bay while Aurora, Northstar, and Jubilee helped out. Phoenix Force Cyclops was also there…

Not one Master Strike showed up, so no Hidden Witnesseses. The Thor/Lady Thor deck was ready to save them all. Dazzler with Piercing Energy and Jubilee with drawing cards made for a lot of lights how. The one escape was due to Rise of the living dead and no bystanders to cover them up.
Second time was much more fun than expected due to my error in the first game having thought ICEMAN was from the x men expansion !🤦🏽‍♂️ 🤣

column2: Mole Man Transforms Commuters Into Giant Ants
column23: Early Master Strike during the game caused Gigantus to escape but after that everything went as good as it could! Right side was focusing on Ant-Man and Beast with Strength/Tech draw combo while Left side used a Captain Marvel & Wonder Man Strength/Ranged strategy that also tucked away all Shang-Chi cards from the HQ (He was the odd man out). On my last turn with the help of Captain Marvel's rare I generated 25 attack which was enough to hit Mole man twice and 2 additional villains!

Why is Shang Chi here? I guess I should've picked heroes to work with him. First game he clogged the hq with shuffling footwork. I felt like I needed x-men to deal with brotherhood effects but I'm worried about the future games when I might need these two.
This was fun, I had some insane combos with a 49 attack hand to end the game where I cleaned up the city and finished off the Mastermind and still had attack left over.

i had a blast with synergies between the chosen heroes,
i had shang chi + jocasta shuffle mechanics (and the shang chi rare appeared at a great time) and
cap marvel+beast+wonder man strength and range synergies;
with also some overlapping on beast and jocasta!
worked out great!
Love this setup! The only thing missing was Sinister clones!
Originally was planning on taking Black Widow but given that their series released on Disney+ today it felt right to take Moonknight.
Between the scheme and Mr Sinister it was hard to keep a hand for most of the game. Nice Challenge

Kitty Pryde and Forge really kept compounding on each other and kept P1 deck very tidy. P2 went heavy into the spiderfriends. even when master strike or scheme twist knocked my hands down I felt I had enough draw to keep playing and manipulate the HQ for the other players.

column2: The Beyonder's Invasion of the Venom Symbiotes
column23: Shards weren't as powerful as all of the Avengers. Having 3 rares on our side definitely helped smack the Beyonder out of the cosmos! On top of that, Adam Warlock ended up being corrupted by power and needed a good smack down as well. Captain America and Hulkbuster happily obliged.

This was quite a challenge, even with great RNG and very late Master Strikes, double henchmen helps with the flow, but with 2 very strong villain groups this can become ugly rather quick, this is our 1st game and we barely saw any Yondu, won by just a few cards left too O_O. Second game had a much stronger start, but my deck slowed to a crawl after a few less than good purchases, leaving my partner to almost solo play the second half into a lame loss, as I was swimming in wounds ._. Phew.
just brutal. Should have gone with Skaar and Fixit, but talked myself out of it....

column2: The Hydra High Council Splices Humans with Spider DNA

column23: Used Undercover instead of KO, which worked better than I thought it would.

Jean Grey was the best choice, for sure! Her bystander saves were top notch. And then the ability to pull piercing from both Banshee and Polaris definitely made things easier. Our second game we scored a considerable amount more than the first, but it was also higher turns and more escapes, so we went with game one score.

This was a tough setup. Deathbird's master strikes and mastermind tactics kept entering the city. Scheme twists and wounds were passed back and forth between players. We found ourselves relying on Banshee and Polaris's piercing energy . It was a challenge, but we did pretty well all things considered!

Wound-mageddon is real. There was also a lot of variability early on. First game was devastating with nearly all schemes coming out at once in the first half. Second game went much better with only a few schemes at the start and early Bishops KO-ing the angry mobs. Left hand was focused on piercing and right hand focused on recruit with Silver Surfer. Hero picks: I wouldn't be surprised if everyone picks Banshee. The covert/ranged triggers and piercing meshed really well. I thought Iceman would do better than he did but I also didn't see his uncommon until the end of the game. If I could choose again, I would have kept Banshee along with someone that KOs really well. Even with a better start, the second game dragged and ended with barely any wound deck and only one card in the villain deck. I'm really surprised I was able to win and luck was definitely a factor.

I really hate this scheme! So many twist, with Deathbird's Master Strike even more wounds! I rather choose blue and red heroes for KOing the Scheme Twists, than KOing the Wounds with example Wolverine..And lots of Schemes came very quickly. Man i hate this stuff!

Left hand got to live the dream. A Str/Inst deck got to finish Thanos with Iron Fist and Drax ultimates together for 2 hits. Wow. Right hand was a crafty tech deck featuring Rocket and Punisher that KOd every grey card (almost too quickly!). Overall got very luck with Strike and Twist timing.

column2: Stryfe Invades the Daily Bugle News HQ
column23: BFG was in the initial HQ so MCU side was dedicated to grabbing Reboots and all the tech triggers to get it going.  Comic side was pure Black Widow and ended up securing the win when Stryfe's final tactic was Psychic Torment letting me shuffle and find the Covert Operation I had bought that turn for a +13 attack.
Player 1started out good then at the end he did nothing.

It's very rare that I play with Black Widow and Angel just because it seems a bit unfair lol. I loved seeing the bystander count ramp up. Nick fury played well in both hands. Black Cat and Scarlet Spider seemed like the right pair for the other hand but I probably should have went with Spider woman. Especially to avoid the wounds from the Enemies Of Asgard. I was worried about not having an X Force hero in my deck but didn't hinder me too much.
It was a lot tougher than I expected
